---
name: frontend-design
description: "Use when improving UI/UX in React or web frontend: visual polish, layout, spacing, typography, responsive behavior, color systems, modern component styling, accessibility tweaks, loading/empty states, and interaction micro-improvements."
---

# Frontend Design Skill

## Goal
Help improve frontend quality with practical, implementation-ready UI decisions.

## Use When
- The user asks to make UI look better or more modern.
- The user asks to improve spacing, alignment, hierarchy, or readability.
- The user asks for responsive improvements for mobile/desktop.
- The user asks for better visual feedback (hover/focus/active/loading states).
- The user asks for consistency across components and pages.

## Workflow
1. Inspect existing structure and keep current design language unless user asks for a redesign.
2. Propose small, high-impact changes first (spacing, typography scale, button/input consistency).
3. Ensure accessibility basics: visible focus, contrast, semantic labels.
4. Add responsive behavior for key breakpoints.
5. Validate no regressions and keep changes scoped.

## Constraints
- Prefer minimal, targeted edits over broad rewrites.
- Preserve existing APIs and behavior unless explicitly requested.
- Avoid introducing new dependencies for simple CSS/UI improvements.

## Output Style
- Explain the visual intent briefly.
- Reference affected files and what changed.
- Include any trade-offs if relevant.
